perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall appoint within its organization a person responsible for the treatment of confidential information and data, who shall have a ll persons in charge of carrying out PROJECT activities sign a declaration, whereby they take it upon themselves to comply with confidentiality obligations as described and regulated in the prese nt AGREEMENT .
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party may, at their discretion, appoint a person responsible for the treatment of confidential information and data. This appointed person is encouraged to have all persons in charge of carrying out PROJECT activities acknowledge the confidentiality obligations as described in the agreement, but a signed declaration is not mandatory.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the appointment of a responsible person and requires a signed declaration from all involved personnel. The changed text makes the appointment discretionary and removes the mandatory requirement for a signed declaration. This creates an in-text contradiction because the level of enforcement is now ambiguous. The obligation to ensure compliance with the agreement becomes less certain due to this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party may, at their discretion, appoint a person responsible for the treatment of confidential information and data. This appointed person is encouraged to have all persons in charge of carrying out PROJECT activities acknowledge the confidentiality obligations as described in the agreement, but a signed declaration is not mandatory. The persons responsible for the abovementioned conf idential data treatment are: - for ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER …………….……………………………….. - for the COMPANY…………………………………………………………………………………. 6. Security measures
perturbation.justification: The changed text makes the appointment of a responsible person discretionary and removes the mandatory requirement for a signed declaration, while the contradicted text assumes the existence of such a person ('The persons responsible for the abovementioned conf idential data treatment are:'). This creates a direct contradiction as one section implies a mandatory role with assigned individuals, while the modified section makes the role optional. This creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The present AGREEMENT does not grant either of THE PARTIES rights or authorization to claim a licence or any other right of use for patents, br ands, models and/or industrial or intellectual property of any kind.
perturbation.changed_text: The present AGREEMENT may grant either of THE PARTIES limited rights of use for patents, brands, models and/or industrial or intellectual property of any kind, subject to a separate written agreement specifying the terms of such use.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly denies granting any rights to intellectual property. The changed text introduces the possibility of granting limited rights with a separate agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction as it is unclear whether the default is no rights or if rights can be granted later, and under what conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Intellectual property The present AGREEMENT may grant either of THE PARTIES limited rights of use for patents, brands, models and/or industrial or intellectual property of any kind, subject to a separate written agreement specifying the terms of such use. 8. Duration Confidentiality obligations are intended to last fr om the signing of the present AGREEMENT up to five years after completion of the PROJECT , currently envisaged for March 16, 2015, except any further extensions of the validity period of this AGREEMENT to be agreed upon by THE PARTIES and before the aforementioned deadline.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly denies granting any rights to intellectual property, while the changed text introduces the possibility of granting limited rights with a separate agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, as it is unclear whether the default is no rights or if rights can be granted later, and under what conditions. The changed text, stating the AGREEMENT 'may grant' rights directly contradicts the original stance of no rights, creating an inconsistency as defined in the provided Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: All communications between THE PARTIES shall be add ressed to the following addresses: - for ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER …………….……………………………….. - for the COMPANY…………………………………………………………………………………. (Place and date) ……………… SIGNATURE OF THE LEGAL REPRESENTATIVE ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER (Name and office) ……………………… SIGNATURE OF THE LEGAL REPRESENTATIVE COMPANY (Name and office) ………………………
perturbation.changed_text: All formal communications between THE PARTIES shall be addressed to the following addresses: - for ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER …………….……………………………….. - for the COMPANY…………………………………………………………………………………. Informal communications may be conducted through any mutually agreed upon channels. (Place and date) ……………… SIGNATURE OF THE LEGAL REPRESENTATIVE ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER (Name and office) ……………………… SIGNATURE OF THE LEGAL REPRESENTATIVE COMPANY (Name and office) ………………………
perturbation.explanation: The original clause stipulates that all communications shall be addressed to the listed addresses. By introducing 'informal communications' that can happen through other channels creates an in-text contradiction as it is not clear if a communication has to be made on the listed addresses or other channels, or both. The contradiction resides in what is the correct and legal way to communicate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Communications All formal communications between THE PARTIES shall be addressed to the following addresses: - for ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER …………….……………………………….. - for the COMPANY…………………………………………………………………………………. Informal communications may be conducted through any mutually agreed upon channels. (Place and date) ……………… SIGNATURE OF THE LEGAL REPRESENTATIVE ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER (Name and office) ……………………… SIGNATURE OF THE LEGAL REPRESENTATIVE COMPANY (Name and office) ………………………
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all communications shall be addressed to specific addresses. The changed text introduces the concept of 'informal communications' that can occur through other channels. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether all communications, including formal and informal ones, must adhere to the specified addresses, or if informal communications are exempt. This ambiguity directly conflicts with the initial 'all communications' requirement and thus constitutes a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For the purpose of this AGREEMENT , those data and information will be considered as confidential which are provided or delivered by one Party to th e other Party and which are declared confidential when provided or delivered.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purpose of this AGREEMENT, all data and information exchanged between the Parties will be considered confidential, regardless of whether it is explicitly declared as such, unless it is already in the public domain or explicitly designated as non-confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stipulates that data and information must be 'declared confidential when provided or delivered' to be considered confidential. The changed text states that *all* data is confidential unless it is public or explicitly designated as non-confidential. This creates an in-text contradiction because it alters the criteria for determining what constitutes confidential information. One section requires an explicit declaration, while the other implies an assumption of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purpose of this AGREEMENT, all data and information exchanged between the Parties will be considered confidential, regardless of whether it is explicitly declared as such, unless it is already in the public domain or explicitly designated as non-confidential. 3.2 The confidential nature of the abovementioned data and information will have to be explicitly declared, printing or writing ‘CONFIDENTIAL’ on the above, followed by the signatures of the representatives of THE PARTIES or, however, clearly indicated as confidential in communications, so to allow THE PARTIES and any authorized person t o immediately recognize their confidential nature and apply the subsequent compliance to confi dentiality obligations as envisaged in this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text requires explicit declaration for data to be considered confidential. The changed text states that *all* exchanged data is confidential unless explicitly non-confidential or public. This directly contradicts section 3.2 in the 'Contradicted Text' that explicitly mandates a clear declaration (e.g., printing 'CONFIDENTIAL' with signatures) for data to be considered confidential. The two clauses are incompatible, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
